title: Hoollongapar Gibbon Sanctuary
tagline: Hoollongapar Gibbon Sanctuary — wildlife reserve of Assam

overview.short: The Hollongapar Gibbon Sanctuary, formerly known as the Gibbon Wildlife Sanctuary or Hollongapar Reserved Forest, is an isolated protected area of evergreen forest located in Assam
overview.description: The Hollongapar Gibbon Sanctuary, formerly known as the Gibbon Wildlife Sanctuary or Hollongapar Reserved Forest, is an isolated protected area of evergreen forest located in Assam, India. The sanctuary was officially constituted and renamed in 1997. Set aside initially in 1881, its forests used to extend to the foothills of the Patkai mountain range. Since then, the forest has been fragmented and surrounded by tea gardens and small villages.

topPlaces.name: Mariani, Jorhat
topPlaces.description: Mariani (IPA: ˌmɑːrɪˈænɪ) is a neighbourhood town of Jorhat, on the border of Nagaland and about 17.5 km from Jorhat Town railway station.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Hoollongapar Gibbon Sanctuary?
faq.a: The best time to visit Hoollongapar Gibbon Sanctuary is Oct – Mar. Summers are 25–42°C and winters are 7–24°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Hoollongapar Gibbon Sanctuary?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Jorhat Airport (21 km) and the nearest railway station is Dimapur Railway Station (111 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Hoollongapar Gibbon Sanctuary?
faq.a: 2 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Hoollongapar Gibbon Sanctuary at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Hoollongapar Gibbon Sanctuary?
faq.a: Stays in Hoollongapar Gibbon Sanctuary start from ₹2,500 per night, with options across 5 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Hoollongapar Gibbon Sanctuary?
faq.a: The top attractions are Mariani, Jorhat.
